topic: ACP-based CLI Agent Wrapping, Permission Guard, and Custom Theme Engine
context: We researched the fourth reference library, DevAgent TUI, to refine our CLI GUI wrapper, action permission controls, and UI customization.
options_considered: Run CLIs inside a raw xterm terminal and parse text stream using regex.

Implement an Agent Communication Protocol (ACP) adapter using JSON-RPC over stdio to programmatically communicate with CLI agents, build a structured Permission Guard (YOLO / Confirm-once / Deny always), and provide a dynamic Custom CSS/Theme Engine for UI personalization.
chosen_option: Implement an ACP JSON-RPC adapter, structured Permission Guard, and dynamic CSS/Theme Engine.
reasoning: ACP Integration: Parsing JSON-RPC stdio streams from agents (like Claude Code, Codex) is much more robust than regex-parsing terminal characters. It allows us to render native UI components for plans, file edits (showing visual diffs), and model switching.

Permission Guard: Developers need security controls when running autonomous CLI agents. Implementing granular states (YOLO, Ask on Write, Ask on Execute) ensures safety while maintaining a smooth developer experience.
Theme Engine: Since OMNIX DevFlow targets a premium developer audience, offering custom CSS overrides and standard editor themes (Dracula, Nord, Cyberpunk) matches our visual excellence guidelines.
tradeoffs: Non-ACP compliant CLIs will fall back to raw terminal execution (wrapped in xterm.js), ensuring general compatibility while providing rich visual overlays for compliant agents.
impact_scope: Frontend rendering layer (CSS theme builder, file diff viewer)
future_risks: Changes in third-party CLI communication formats might break JSON-RPC parsing: resolved by supporting lenient fallback to raw stdio stream output.
